title: World’s 1st nailable solar shingle offers 23% more power with upgrade
description: GAF Energy’s Timberline Solar ES 2 generates 23% more power than its predecessor and offers better installation efficiency.
bodyText: A New Jersey-based firm has launched the latest upgrade of the world’s first nailable solar shingle. GAF Energy’s Timberline Solar ES 2 reportedly delivers 23% more power than its predecessor, offering enhanced installation efficiency and expanded design compatibility. According to the company, ES 2 boasts an industry-defying depth of less than a quarter inch and integrates with traditional GAF shingles to create a sleek and attractive look. In 2022, GAF launched the world’s first nailable solar shingle. Solar shingles, or photovoltaic shingles, are solar panels that look and work like traditional roofing materials. The company’s Timberline Solar meets a host of rigorous third-party safety standards covering both roofing and solar. ES 2 is claimed to deliver more power, superior installation, and enhanced design. “Three years ago, we transformed the solar industry with Timberline Solar. Today, we’re raising the bar again,” said Martin DeBono, President of GAF Energy. “Timberline Solar ES 2 represents everything we’ve learned about solar roofing, enhanced by extensive feedback from contractors and homeowners. It’s an innovation that delivers a solar shingle that is more powerful, even easier to install, and more versatile than ever before.” The company has revealed that the Timberline Solar ES 2 builds on GAF Energy’s breakthrough approach to solar roofing. The ES 2 delivers 57 watts of power per shingle, a 23% increase in energy generation. It also enables superior installation speed and offers more area than a traditional asphalt shingle. The ES 2 also offers enhanced StrikeZone for improved installation efficiency and is compatible with the complete GAF Timberline shingle collection. In November, ES2’s predecessor was approved for installation by the Sterling Ranch master-planned community. The acceptance suggested that Timberline Solar meets or exceeds the community’s high safety, energy production, and aesthetics standards. The availability of Timberline Solar is expected to help Sterling Ranch reach its sustainability targets, including achieving 100% renewable electricity by 2030. The company recently completed a 450,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Georgetown, Texas. GAF Energy’s Timberline Solar can help homeowners as they will no longer have to compromise with their looks when adding solar power to their roofs. Timberline Solar is made up of shingles, not panels or heavy tiles. The company has revealed that these shingles are water-shedding, strong, and warranted to withstand winds up to 130 mph. Rack-mounted solar installations—where the solar is separate from the roof—require drilling dozens of holes into the roof membrane. Roof warranties do not typically cover any resulting damage related to those holes. Prabhat, an alumnus of the Indian Institute of Mass Communication, is a tech and defense journalist. While he enjoys writing on modern weapons and emerging tech, he has also reported on global politics and business. He has been previously associated with well-known media houses, including the International Business Times (Singapore Edition) and ANI.
